name: setuptools-dso

metadata.name: setuptools-dso
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: setuptools extension to build non-python shared libraries

documentation:
# setuptools extension for building non-Python Dynamic Shared Objects

Building non-python shared libraries (eg. `libY.so`, `libY.dylib`, or `Y.dll`) for inclusion in a Python Wheel.

This extension is an alternative to bundling externally built
libraries in Python Wheel packages by providing the means to
replace an external build system (eg. Makefile) so that non-python
libraries to be built from source within the python ecosystem.

- Documentation at https://mdavidsaver.github.io/setuptools_dso
- Github project https://github.com/mdavidsaver/setuptools_dso
- PYPI https://pypi.org/project/setuptools-dso/

functions.name: setuptools_dso.setup
documentation:
setup(..., x_dsos=[DSO(...)])
Wrapper around setuptools.setup() which injects extra Commands needed to build DSOs.
Unknown keywords are passed through.

```
param x_dsos

None, a list of  instances, or a callable with one argument returning such a list.
```

functions.name: setuptools_dso._cythonize
documentation:
Dummy cythonize() used when Cython not installed.
Assumes that generated source files are distributed.
Cython docs say "It is strongly recommended that you
distribute the generated .c ...".

functions.name: setuptools_dso.cythonize
documentation:
Wrapper around Cython.Build.cythonize() to correct handling of
{py:class}`DSO` s and {py:class}`Extension` s using them.

documentation: Copyright 2022  Michael Davidsaver
SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD
See LICENSE

functions.name: setuptools_dso.compiler._patch_fix_compile_args
documentation: workaround for https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3591
cf. https://github.com/mdavidsaver/setuptools_dso/issues/23
setuptools >=63.4.3
each call to CCompiler.compile(..., include_dirs=None) appends to the
self.include_dirs list, growing without bound.
With MSVC this eventually fails.

functions.name: setuptools_dso.compiler._default_preprocess
documentation:
preprocess() is documented to raise PreprocessError but the
default (circa py3.9) impl. is a no-op, and the 'unix' impl.
raises CompileError.  Always a pleasure to find accurate
documentation...

functions.name: setuptools_dso.compiler._msvc_preprocess
documentation: Replacement for default (no-op) distutils.msvccompiler.MSVCCompiler.preprocess()

functions.name: setuptools_dso.compiler.new_compiler
documentation: Returns a instance of a sub-class of distutils.ccompiler.CCompiler

classes.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.compile
documentation:
Compile provided source code and return path to resulting object file

:returns: Path string to object file in temporary location.
:param str src: Source code string
:param str language: Source code language: 'c' or 'c++'
:param list define_macros: Extra macro definitions.
:param list include_dirs: Extra directories to search for headers
:param list extra_preargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler
:param list extra_postargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.try_compile
documentation:
Return True if provided source code compiles

:param str src: Source code string
:param str language: Source code language: 'c' or 'c++'
:param list define_macros: Extra macro definitions.
:param list include_dirs: Extra directories to search for headers
:param list extra_preargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler
:param list extra_postargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.check_includes
documentation:
Return true if all of the headers may be included (in order)

:param list headers: List of header file names
:param str language: Source code language: 'c' or 'c++'
:param list define_macros: Extra macro definitions.
:param list include_dirs: Extra directories to search for headers
:param list extra_preargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler
:param list extra_postargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.check_include
documentation:
Return true if the header may be included

:param str header: Header file name
:param str language: Source code language: 'c' or 'c++'
:param list define_macros: Extra macro definitions.
:param list include_dirs: Extra directories to search for headers
:param list extra_preargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler
:param list extra_postargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.sizeof
documentation:
Return size in bytes of provided typename

:param str typename: Header file name
:param list headers: List of headers to include during all test compilations
:param str language: Source code language: 'c' or 'c++'
:param list define_macros: Extra macro definitions.
:param list include_dirs: Extra directories to search for headers
:param list extra_preargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler
:param list extra_postargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.check_symbol
documentation:
Return True if symbol name (macro, variable, or function) is defined/delcared

:param str symname: Symbol name
:param list headers: List of headers to include during all test compilations
:param str language: Source code language: 'c' or 'c++'
:param list define_macros: Extra macro definitions.
:param list include_dirs: Extra directories to search for headers
:param list extra_preargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler
:param list extra_postargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.check_member
documentation:
Return True if the given structure has the named member

:param str struct: Structure name
:param str member: Member name
:param list headers: List of headers to include during all test compilations
:param str language: Source code language: 'c' or 'c++'
:param list define_macros: Extra macro definitions.
:param list include_dirs: Extra directories to search for headers
:param list extra_preargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler
:param list extra_postargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.eval_macros
documentation:
Expand C/C++ preprocessor macros.

For undefined macros, None is returned.
For defined macros a string is returned.
When evaluating multiple macros, the order of the macros argument is preserved
in the OrderedDict which is returned.

:returns: An OrderedDict mapping to string (if defined) or None (if not defined)
:param str|list macros: A macro name string, or a list of such strings
:param list headers: List of headers to include during all test compilations
:param list define_macros: Extra macro definitions.
:param str language: Source code language: 'c' or 'c++'  (Added in 2.9)
:param list include_dirs: Extra directories to search for headers
:param list extra_preargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler
:param list extra_postargs: Extra arguments to pass to the compiler

methods.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ProbeToolchain.info
documentation:
Inspect toolchain

```
returns

A 
```

Inspection of compiler

:param bool verbose: If True, enable additional prints
:param str compiler: If not None, select non-default compiler toolchain
:param list headers: List of headers to include during all test compilations
:param list define_macros: List of (macro, value) tuples to define during all test compilations

classes.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo

class_variables.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo.compiler_type
documentation:
Directly copied from {py:class}`distutils.ccompiler.CCompiler.compiler_type`

Known values include: 'bcpp', 'cygwin', 'mingw', 'msvc', 'unix'

class_variables.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo.compiler
documentation:
Compiler implementation name

Possible values; 'clang', 'gcc', 'msvc'

class_variables.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo.compiler_version
documentation:
Compiler release version as a tuple of integers suitible for comparison

eg. for GCC: (4,9,2), clang: (11,0,1), msvc: (19,0,24245)

class_variables.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo.gnuish
documentation: True when compiler is clang or gcc

class_variables.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo.target_os
documentation:
Target OS runtime environment

Known values: "cygwin", "linux", "osx", "windows"

class_variables.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo.target_arch
documentation:
Target CPU architecture

Known values: "aarch64", "arm32", "amd64", "i386"

class_variables.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo.address_width
documentation:
Width in bits of a virtual address.  aka. 8*sizeof(void*)

Known values: 32, 64

class_variables.name: setuptools_dso.probe.ToolchainInfo.endian
documentation:
Target byte order for multi-byte values

Known values: "little", "big"
Information about a compiler toolchain

modules.name: setuptools_dso.runtime
documentation: Copyright 2022  Michael Davidsaver
SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD
See LICENSE
Tools for interacting with packages containing DSOs w/ dsoinfo modules
as built with setuptools_dso >= 2

functions.name: setuptools_dso.runtime._dso2info
documentation:
Return mangled name of DSO info module.

eg. 'my.pkg.libs.adso' -> 'my.pkg.libs.adso_dsoinfo'

functions.name: setuptools_dso.runtime.import_dsoinfo
documentation:
Import and return "info" module for the named DSO.

:param str dso: DSO name string (eg. 'my.pkg.libs.adso').
:param str package: Package name to resolve relative imports.  cf. importlib.import_module
:returns: Info module

For example, on a ELF target the "info" module for a DSO "mypkg.lib.thelib" would contain the attributes:

- `.dsoname`    eg. "mypkg.lib.thelib"
- `.libname`    eg. "thelib.so"
- `.soname`     eg. "thelib.so.0"
- `.filename`   eg. "/full/path/to/thelib.so"
- `.sofilename` eg. "/full/path/to/thelib.so.0"

functions.name: setuptools_dso.runtime.dylink_prepare_dso
documentation:
Take steps necessary to allow the named DSO to be loaded implicitly.

eg. On Windows, call `os.add_dll_directory()` as neeeded.

:param str dso: DSO name string (eg. 'my.pkg.libs.adso').
:param str package: Package name to resolve relative imports.  cf. importlib.import_module
:returns: Info module for the named DSO

functions.name: setuptools_dso.runtime.find_dso
documentation:
Lookup DSO file name. eg. for use with ctypes

```
param str dso

DSO name string (eg. 'my.pkg.libs.adso').

param str package

Package name to resolve relative imports.  cf. importlib.import_module

param bool so

When True (default) return SO qualified name.
eg. "libblah.so.0" vs. "libblah.so".
No effect on Windows.

returns

Absolute path string of DSO file.
```

eg.

```
fname = setuptools_dso.find_dso('my.pkg.libs.adso')
lib = ctypes.CDLL(fname, ctypes.RTLD_GLOBAL)
```

modules.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd
documentation: Copyright 2022  Michael Davidsaver
SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD
See LICENSE

functions.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.system_concurrency
documentation:
Estimate the number of concurrent compile jobs this system can perform.

Tries to use both available CPU and memory resource information.

functions.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.massage_dir_list
documentation:
Process a list of directories for use with -I or -L
For relative paths, also include paths relative to a build directory

functions.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.has_dsos
documentation: Rant:
Arranging for 'build_dso' to be run is seriously annoying!
distutils presents such a simple idea.  We just add 'build_dso' to build.sub_commands.
'install' will first run 'build', and we're done for everything except 'bdist_egg'.
Enter setuptools...  setuptools decides to automagically alias 'install' as 'bdist_egg'.
Seriously 'magic', it's inspecting the strack frames...
'bdist_egg' is a special little snowflake (aka. total hack) which doesn't run 'build'
or 'install' (fuck!), but does it's own thing by piecemeal calling
eg. 'egg_info', 'build_clib' (conditionally), 'install_lib'.
so what to do...
1. insert 'build_dso' before 'build_clib' in the 'build' sequence.
This handles everything except 'bdist_egg' (aka. 'setup.py install')...
2. override 'bdist_egg' to run 'build_dso' at an appropriate point.

functions.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd._needs_builddso
documentation: _needs_builddso marks distutils/setuptools command to depend on build_dso.
if right_before is specified build_dso is injected before that command
instead of as first dependency.

classes.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.Pool
documentation: bypass multiprocessing optimization (parallel compile)
for older py (can't pickle the necessary pieces)
freeze_support() is a pain, and is needed by methods other than 'fork'.
Contrary to documentation, this is not limited to Windows.
https://bugs.python.org/issue32146
https://bugs.python.org/issue33725

classes.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.Extension
documentation:
Extension(name, sources, ..., dsos=[DSO(...)])
Wrapper around setuptools.Extension which accepts a list of {py:class}`DSO` dependencies.

```
param dsos

A list of  s which this Extension will be linked against.
```

classes.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.DSO
documentation:
DSO(name, sources, ..., dsos=[DSO(...)], soversion=None)
A Dynamic Shared Object to be built.
Accepts the same options as {py:class}`Extension` with additions:

```
param dsos

A list of  s which this DSO will be linked against.

param str soversion

None (default) or a string.  On supported targerts, this string
which will be appended to create the SONAME.  eg. soversion="0"
will turn libfoo.so into libfoo.so.0.

param dict lang_compile_args

Language specific ("c" or "c++") compiler flags.
eg. {'c':['-DMAGIC']}

param str gen_info

Controls generation of "info" module.
True (default) uses the conventional filename,
False disables generation,
or a specific filename string.
```

methods.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.build_dso._name2file
documentation:
Translate DSO name (eg. "pkg.mod.mylib" into
"pkg/mod/mylib.so" or (if so==True) "pkg/mod/mylib.so.0"

methods.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.build_dso._name2libname
documentation:
Translate DSO name to library name

For example, DSO "mylib" on Linux would return "libmylib.so".

classes.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.build
documentation: hack...
setuptools/distutils decides to treat build as a "purelib" vs. "platlib"
by testing 'dist.ext_modules' (not call 'dist.has_ext_modules()' mind you...)
So we can't simply patch has_ext_modules() to also check for DSOs.
Otherwise a package contains DSOs, but not Extensions, would incorrectly
be treated as "purelib".

classes.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.bdist_wheel
documentation:
Since 'auditwheel' doesn't understand the idea of non-python libraries in the python tree,
we provide an alternate way to handling changing the platform tag on Linux by environment
variable.  eg.

$ SETUPTOOLS_DSO_PLAT_NAME=manylinux2014_x86_64 pip install .

methods.name: setuptools_dso.dsocmd.bdist_egg.run_command
documentation: An ugly hack on top of an ugly hack...
we can't hook into 'egg_info' unconditionally, as other targets like 'sdist'
don't want to build things...
so instead we hook in and run 'build_dso' just after 'egg_info'.
